title: Erdős #349 assembly piece: the pair (1, 2) is good
informal_statement: One of the four assembly pieces for the integer_isGoodPair_iff characterization used toward Erdos Problem #349: the pair (1, 2) is good -- eventually (for all sufficiently large integers k) k is a finite sum of distinct elements of the range of n |-> floor(1 * 2^n) = 2^n. This is a sublemma supporting the integer_isGoodPair_iff characterization, not a resolution of #349 itself (which asks, in general, for which real t, alpha the sequence floor(t*alpha^n) is additively complete -- still genuinely OPEN).
informal_proof_idea: For any k >= 0, let E be the set of bit positions of k.toNat (binary representation). The image of E under n |-> 2^n gives a Finset of distinct powers of 2 in the range of the target sequence (since floor(1*2^i) = 2^i exactly), and their sum equals k.toNat = k, using injectivity of n |-> 2^n to transfer the sum through Finset.image.
formal_statement_pp: theorem erdos_349_one_two_is_good_pair : ∀ᶠ k in Filter.atTop, k ∈ {n : ℤ | ∃ B : Finset ℤ, ↑B ⊆ Set.range (fun n : ℕ ↦ ⌊(1:ℝ) * (2:ℝ) ^ n⌋) ∧ n = ∑ i ∈ B, i}

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop; root_statement_hash confirmed byte-identical to the upstream source hash cited in the sibling repo's own provenance comment

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only one of four infrastructure sublemmas toward the integer_isGoodPair_iff characterization for Erdos #349's integer case -- it does not resolve #349's general open question. Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode a6b56b68, problem_version eefd30ca-3d52-4c1e-beff-1e463d130113), a single Solve step (proof_format raw_lean_block to preserve the two-goal `refine`/bullet structure), kernel_verified on the first attempt, well within the environment's 60-second wall-clock cap. Notably, a closely related target attempted earlier this cycle -- the dyadic alpha=2 fiber sublemma (floor((1/2^k)*2^n), needing field_simp/push_cast reasoning over a division) -- timed out twice at ~60s on the identical proof shape minus the division; recorded as a blocker in BLOCKERS.md. This packet's simpler `1 * 2^n` (no division) case elaborates comfortably within the time cap, suggesting the field_simp/division reasoning specifically was the slow part, not general Mathlib elaboration overhead. root_statement_hash confirmed byte-identical to the hash cited in the sibling repo's own source comment. Independently re-verified in THIS environment's own pinned toolchain rather than treated as evidence merely because the sibling repo (F:\Github\mnehmos.llm-driven-proof-search.environment\ErdosProblems\erdos-349\proof\Erdos349_one_two_isGoodPair.lean) already reports it kernel_verified there -- per this repo's evidence policy. training.eligibility: quarantined per the default-quarantine policy for open_problem_related packets pending real external review. Fifth packet in the frontier/erdos lane; 2/7 of the integer_isGoodPair_iff cluster now packetized.
